creator: Vladimir.Chukharev@tut.fi
text:
On Mon, 04 Jul 2011 08:23:08 +0300, Vladimir Chukharev <Vladimir.Chukharev@tut.fi> wrote:

> On Sun, 03 Jul 2011 15:42:43 +0300, Max Brazhnikov <makc@issp.ac.ru> wrote:
>
>> Could you check you scilab dependencies with 'pkg_info -g'? I remember I had
>> similar error, if not exactly the same, and the culprit was corrupted package
>> for one of xml* ports.
>>
>> Max
>>
>
> Thanks for the suggestion, I can do that.
...
> I will reinstall gettext, hdf-java and/or junit and come back tomorrow.

Deleting junit and re-installing hdf-java and gettext fixes 'pkg_info -g',
but not installation of scilab. After the installation, the same report is
obtained from 'pkg_info -g'.

I'm inclined to think that this is a problem of 32-bit java on a 64-bit
processor. Is there a way to check this convincingly?

-- 
Vladimir Chukharev
Tampere University of Technology
